evidence: Paper uses Gini importance to rank variants: "We used VariantSpark to determine the Gini-importance score for all genetic variants in the viral genome." Main claims lean on ranking: "We found that the mutations clustering around the 3′-5′ exonuclease had the highest importance scores of all the significant pathogenic loci." Corroborated by logistic regression: "The rank correlation for the top 20 LR hits was 0.90, indicating a good agreement on the dominant signal" and robustness tested via balanced dataset replication showing "clusters of mutations in S proteins were reproduced."
